title: Andryala lanata
intro: Andryala lanata, also known as Woolly Andryala, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in soothing digestive issues and reducing the severity of respiratory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Andryala lanata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Andryala lanata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Andryala lanata belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of flowering plants that is native to the Mediterranean region. Within the Asteraceae family, Andryala lanata is part of the tribe Anthemideae. This tribe is further classified into the subfamily Asteroideae. Andryala lanata's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.
